title: delphinium viscosum subsp. gigantobracteum
intro: Delphinium viscosum subsp. gigantobracteum, a lesser-known subspecies of the common delphinium plant, is a flowering perennial native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant, also known as giant-bracted sticky delphinium, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits such as reducing swelling and promoting wound healing. The plant is often used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer relief from various health issues, including fever reduction and digestive problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Delphinium viscosum subsp. gigantobracteum as a medicinal plant, and its use should be approached with caution under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
